title: Postharvest Food Loss Measurement And Reduction Potential In Sub-Saharan Africa
summary: Build a verified workflow for measuring postharvest food loss at sub-national scale in Sub-Saharan Africa, linking crop-production data, storage-infrastructure data, and household-survey food-loss modules.
why_it_matters: FAO estimates 30-40 percent of food produced in Sub-Saharan Africa is lost postharvest — enough to feed 300 million people. Losses concentrate at the farm-to-market stage where storage, transport, and processing infrastructure is weakest. Measuring where losses are highest and what infrastructure would reduce them is the missing link for investment targeting.

success_metrics.target: Every loss estimate links production, storage, and survey data with methodology.

review_policy.merge_gate: Schema validation, domain review, safety review, replication.
